posts.text:
“If only you would buy even more of my stuff and not worry about the downsides, I’d be even richer!”  - Jensen Huang, in slightly more polite words.


Jade Cole (@JadeCole2112)

"The thing we're doing isn't the problem...  it's YOU noticing it..."





— https://nitter.net/JadeCole2112/status/2033989669717766468#m
not everything Hinton says is representative of all or most AI researchers.

IF people understood this study, Nvidia would drop, sharply.


Gary Marcus (@GaryMarcus)

BREAKING: Reliability, which I have been harping on here since 2019, continues to be deep problem, even with the latest models.

A new @Princeton review below offers a taxonomy of some of the many ways in which reliability continues to haunt LLMs seven years and a trillion dollars later.

Crucially, “many models lack metacognition about their own reliability”. They don’t know what they don’t know.  

Forget

BREAKING: Reliability, which I have been harping on here since 2019, continues to be deep problem, even with the latest models.

A new @Princeton review below offers a taxonomy of some of the many ways in which reliability continues to haunt LLMs seven years and a trillion dollars later.

Crucially, “many models lack metacognition about their own reliability”. They don’t know what they don’t know.  

Forget about AGI if you can’t solve that problem.

It’s past time to rethink the whole LLM parad

Correction: OpenAI was *never* about AI for humanity.

They were always about the highest bidder.

They didn’t change. People finally caught on.

Other than that, no notes on the below.


Tuki (@TukiFromKL)

🚨 Let me tell you what OpenAI just did to its own investor.

> Microsoft gave OpenAI $13 billion.. to build them the infrastructure, gave them Azure, and made them what they are.

> OpenAI just signed a deal with AWS - Microsoft's biggest competitor - to sell AI to the US government.

F Cancer

Why has AI had so little impact on Cancer? New essay, link below.

Americans are right.

Unless we change the ways in which we are building and regulating AI, the net impact here will be negative.

It’s not a narrative problem; it’s an unfettered corporate greed problem.


Brad Gerstner (@altcap)

AI is deeply unpopular. According to Pew, sadly only 17% of Americans think AI will have a positive impact.  In China, 83% believe AI will be positive.  A token tax & political backlash is coming unless the narrative changes.  🇺🇸👀🧐



— https://nitter.net/altcap/s
